summary: The additive module, closed dependency seam, legacy CLI preservation, authority/mirror rules, and Gate-only pr_ready transition are well bounded, but the pre-bootstrap creation lock is caller-worktree-local rather than Story-scoped across repository worktrees.
inspection_summary: Inspected the Story, Architecture, 17-clause Spec, test plan, current eight-file diff, guarded session/CLI/Git-identity implementation, codebase-memory impact and call paths, fresh head-bound verification artifacts, and reran the focused guarded-run-session test file (40/40 pass). Managed, repository, source_fallback, mirror-repair, legacy CLI, lifecycle, migration, and typed-error surfaces are covered; the no-legacy alternate-worktree concurrency path is not.
judgment_delta: Initial concern that the new Run Session authority might leak orchestration or mutate legacy commands -> the dedicated module, injected closed dependency set, forbidden-source assertions, additive CLI routing, and exact legacy-status regression test support the intended architecture boundary.

Initial expectation that INV-004/S-008 made bootstrap exclusive per Story -> resolveCreationLockRoot returns caller.root_realpath whenever no legacy state exists, so two linked/unmanaged worktrees of the same repository acquire different lock paths before authority exists; the current tests only unify source and managed callers after legacy state already identifies them.

Regression lens: fresh head-bound evidence reports the full suite passing and the focused 40-test rerun covers legacy CLI, failure, migration, and persistence behavior -> no existing-surface regression was observed, but passing evidence does not exercise the missing alternate-worktree race.

Path/surface lens: repository, managed, source_fallback, repair, implicit selection, human-readable error, and artifact surfaces are explicit -> final verdict remains needs_changes because the pre-bootstrap secondary-worktree input path can bypass the advertised Story-scoped lock and has no pre-fix-failing fixture.

findings.detail: In src/guarded-run-session.js:155-157 and 211-223, a missing legacy execution causes resolveCreationLockRoot() to return caller.root_realpath. Different linked or unmanaged worktrees for the same Git repository therefore create different .vibepro/executions/<story>/.run-creation.lock directories and can both enter startExecution() for the same Story before either authority binding is visible. The existing INV-004 test at test/guarded-run-session.test.js:256-267 only proves lock convergence when preexisting legacy metadata already names source/managed roots. This contradicts the Spec's Story-scoped exclusive bootstrap attribution boundary. Derive the bootstrap lock from a repository-shared canonical identity (for example the Git common directory with an explicit lock storage contract), or fail closed for noncanonical bootstrap callers, and add a concurrent two-linked-worktree/no-legacy regression fixture that fails on the current implementation and proves only one bootstrap can enter.
